title: Protocol: genomics and precision medicine trial activity in Africa
primary_estimand: Count of genomics / precision medicine / pharmacogenomics interventional trials in Africa vs comparator regions
body: This cross-sectional registry study will quantify the absence of genomics and precision medicine interventional trials in Africa using ClinicalTrials.gov. We will query the API v2 for all interventional studies matching genomics, precision medicine, pharmacogenomics, whole genome, exome, or genetic testing, stratified by location across Africa, United States, China, India, Brazil, and the United Kingdom. The primary outcome is the count of registered genomics trials in Africa compared with each comparator region. Secondary outcomes include BRCA, HER2, and biomarker-driven trial counts in Africa, the number of H3Africa-tagged interventional trials reflecting clinical translation of the consortium's genomic discoveries, and pharmacogenomics gene-specific trial counts for CYP2D6, CYP2B6, CYP3A5, UGT1A1, VKORC1, and HLA-B across Africa versus the United States. Individual African country breakdowns will cover South Africa, Nigeria, Kenya, Uganda, and Egypt. All queries, classification rules, and code will be archived with the analysis. No patient-level data will be used. The study is limited by completeness of a single registry.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.

checks.value: Count of registered genomics trials in Africa vs comparator regions
BRCA/HER2/biomarker counts, H3Africa trial count, PGx gene-specific counts (CYP2D6, CYP2B6, CYP3A5, UGT1A1, VKORC1, HLA-B)
United States, China, India, Brazil, United Kingdom
South Africa, Nigeria, Kenya, Uganda, Egypt
genomics OR precision medicine OR pharmacogenomics OR whole genome OR exome OR genetic testing
no patient-level data, public registry only
